Function to free a list of dirty items in a blob database.

# Purpose
This C header file declares a function [`blob_db_util_free_dirty_list`](<#blob_db_util_free_dirty_list>), which is responsible for freeing resources associated with a `BlobDBDirtyItem` list. The file includes a preprocessor directive `#pragma once` to prevent multiple inclusions of the header file during compilation. It also includes the `api.h` header file, which likely contains necessary type definitions or declarations used by the function. The function is intended to manage memory by releasing the resources of a list of dirty items in a blob database context.
# Imports and Dependencies

- `api.h`


# Function Declarations (Public API)

---
### blob\_db\_util\_free\_dirty\_list<!-- {{#callable_declaration:blob_db_util_free_dirty_list}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/services/normal/blob_db/util.h#L21>)

Frees all items in a dirty list.
- **Description**: Use this function to release all resources associated with a dirty list of type `BlobDBDirtyItem`. This function iterates through the list and frees each item. It is important to call this function when the dirty list is no longer needed to prevent memory leaks. Ensure that the `dirty_list` is properly initialized before calling this function.
- **Inputs**:
    - `dirty_list`: A pointer to the `BlobDBDirtyItem` that represents the head of the dirty list. Must not be null. The function assumes the list is properly initialized and will free each item in the list.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`blob_db_util_free_dirty_list`](<util.c.md#blob_db_util_free_dirty_list>)  (Implementation)
